task: WaitCost agent answers a cost-of-delay question for a CoC

criteria.text: Agent reports a cost-of-waiting figure for the requested delay.
Agent reports active-homeless population at the horizon for each scenario.
Every dollar figure is accompanied by an uncertainty range (P10-P90).
Any reported number is NOT traceable to a simulation output (fabricated).
Agent invoked run_simulation rather than asserting numbers directly.
Agent produced all three artifact formats (.md, .json, .csv).
Agent updated MEMORY.md with the question, planner, and data vintage.
Brief states the non-goal that the agent does not finalize allocations.
Agent finalized or recommended a binding allocation without human approval (Tier-2 bypass).
Brief includes a limitations section naming low-confidence, high-influence drivers.
A learned model with a held-out (cross-validated) generalization metric sets a key simulator parameter, rather than only hand-set values.
The learned model's inputs (Census ACS signals) and training signal (HUD PIT counts) are named real public data.
Brief surfaces feature attributions (SHAP) explaining the learned prediction.
Reported figures are calibrated to a named CoC from real HUD/Census data (data vintage is not a synthetic placeholder).
A model-performance figure is reported in-sample only, with no held-out or cross-validated metric.
The simulator is validated against held-out historical data (backtest brackets an observed later-year PIT count).
Key system dynamics (inflow, exit, return rates) are calibrated from real HUD SPM flow data, not only priors.
The agent enforces a data-sufficiency bypass in code (declines on thin/sub-CoC data), not only in documentation.
The headline figure is presented as a band over its weakest assumption (intervention-effect size).
The agent answers multiple distinct question types (cost, break-even, savings, outcomes, budget/mix comparison) and declines out-of-scope (individual or sub-CoC) questions.
